title: dryopteris boivinii
intro: Dryopteris boivinii is a species of fern that is native to Southeast Asia. It is often referred to as Boivin's wood fern or Boivin's shield fern, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Dryopteris boivinii is typically prepared as an infusion or decoction, and can also be used in the form of a tincture or ointment.
